visible_observations: A very large low multi-legged creature combines rib-like cages, pale bone or wooden plates, exposed red cables or vessels, jointed clawed limbs, and a huge open pincer on one side.
A large heart-shaped organ hangs exposed at the center of the chest and another smaller organ sits within the open pincer; tubes and cords connect them through the body.
The low spider-crab stance, numerous sharpened feet, open cutting claw, exposed organs, and skeletal shell make predation and bodily ruin immediately visible.
defects: The rib cages, plates, cables, vessels, hooks, joints, hearts, and repeated carved textures overlap so densely that locomotion and individual limb attachment points are difficult to follow.
Pale shell surfaces read variably as bone, wood, aged metal, or stone, leaving the material construction inconsistent.
The central heart and side pincer compete as two major focal points without a visible head or gaze to establish attack direction.
Repeated ribs, wires, organic folds, and engraved plates create uniform high-frequency detail across nearly the entire body.
uncertainties: The image does not reveal whether the central heart is a face, core, weak point, passenger, or exposed organ.
No conventional eyes or head are visible, so the creature's current attention and target cannot be determined.
The exact number of legs and whether the pincer contains a separate organism remain unresolved because of overlap.

visible_observations: Three labeled candidates show a tall narrow stilt creature with an open rib-cage body, a low wide many-legged creature with a large side pincer, and a tall armored spider-beetle body with two long front blades.
The candidates differ substantially in height, width, locomotion, armor mass, negative space, and front-weapon arrangement while retaining an exposed central organ.
defects: Dense interior linework rather than pure outer contour carries much of the rib cage and organ identity.
The selected-looking low candidate has many overlapping legs under one broad shell, producing a congested lower silhouette.
The tall candidates and low pincer candidate imply materially different movement and attack behavior, leaving few stable features beyond the central organ and skeletal construction.
uncertainties: The A, B, and C labels do not explicitly state which candidate was selected.
It is unclear whether the various front blades and pincer forms are alternate limbs of one species or entirely different body mechanisms.

visible_observations: The crop shows rib-like arches, layered shell plates, bundled red vessels, several jointed limbs, and part of the large exposed heart-shaped organ.
No eyes, mouth, brows, head, or other clear facial feature appear in the crop; the organ and surrounding cage occupy the attention area.
defects: There is no visible gaze, facial asymmetry, mouth state, or directional sensory organ from which to infer present attention.
The heart shape communicates exposed vulnerability or organic machinery but not a specific current emotional or interpersonal state.
Dense ribs, cables, and repeated surface textures obscure which structure functions as the creature's attention mechanism.
uncertainties: Unaided viewing cannot determine whether the organ is conscious, whether another hidden head exists, or how the creature perceives a target.
The crop does not establish whether pulsing, movement, or other non-facial behavior supplies the intended attention read.

visible_observations: At native size, the broad low shell, central dark organ area, multiple front legs, and large pale side pincer remain visible.
The body reads as a crab-, spider-, or scorpion-like monster with one dominant cutting claw.
defects: The exposed heart, secondary organ, vessels, rib construction, individual joints, material distinctions, and exact leg count collapse at this size.
Several legs and underside cables merge into one dark lower mass.
The surviving outline communicates a generic armored arthropod more strongly than the specific organ-and-cage construction.
uncertainties: No neighboring unit icons, gameplay background, selection frame, or motion state are shown, so in-context recognition, facing direction, and pincer readability cannot be determined.
